title: Six things that bit me migrating an observability pipeline
excerpt: Six bugs from a recent migration of 30-day SLA reporting off Splunk Summary Indexing onto a Lambda pipeline that aggregates inside Coralogix and emits to Datadog. Each one is a layer that didn't say what it actually meant: the platform accepted the write but didn't store it, the 403 didn't mention scope, the compile error blamed the wrong line, the retry was invisible to the function.

title: Why we didn't reach for Kafka
excerpt: A colleague asked, half-jokingly, whether we'd considered Kafka for a reporting pipeline. Kafka would have given us a substrate to operate without changing the primitive the pipeline actually depends on. Queues solve message durability; we needed outcome durability. A different problem with a differently-shaped answer, and a 1:1 mapping that surprised me when I drew it out.

title: Self-healing pipelines need a human in the loop
excerpt: Auto-recovery is the default the cloud-vendor blog posts sell, and it works for transient failures. On deterministic failures it generates retry storms that drown the logs. On systemic failures it amplifies the outage. Three failure classes, three different right answers, and a classification gate small enough that the implementation more or less writes itself.

title: Shipping fresh CSS after deploy: lessons from a stale-client debugging session
excerpt: Chasing a broken-layout bug across an Angular 21 SSG + service-worker stack and the four lessons that fell out: why content-hashed assets aren't enough, the SwUpdate plus build-stamp belt-and-braces guard, sidebar collapse geometry, and why heading permalinks are a desktop-only affordance.

title: Motion you can feel: notes from a 25-finding audit
excerpt: What I learned auditing every animation, transition, and micro-interaction in my Angular 21 portfolio: a six-token motion ladder, transform: scaleX over width, View Transitions for routes and theme, and a reduced-motion contract you can grade against.

title: Storage on Purpose: Building the NAS Tier of My Homelab
excerpt: Part 1 of the homelab series: a storage-first design for the stateful layer. A UGREEN DXP4800 Plus on UGOS Pro, a RAID 5 bulk pool over WD Red Plus WD80EFPX CMR disks, two local Samsung 990 EVO Plus V8 NVMe pools for Immich and app data, and a US3000 DC UPS. The point is not a faster box; it is a durable boundary between permanent data and disposable compute.

title: Taming State with Angular Signals
excerpt: Practical patterns for replacing BehaviorSubjects with Signals—service state, computed derivations, SSR safety, and when to still reach for RxJS.

title: Building Cloud-Native CI/CD Pipelines
excerpt: A hands-on walkthrough of designing resilient, container-native CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, Docker, and Kubernetes—from build triggers to zero-downtime deployments.

title: Blog Post Template
excerpt: A ready-to-copy template that demonstrates all supported Markdown features: headings, code blocks, lists, blockquotes, and more.
